title: Shikigami Character Manifest v1.0
description: Metadata and state→asset mapping for a Shikigami character package (.shikigami). See docs/adr/003-character-package-format.md and docs/TDD.md §3.3.

id.description: Stable unique identifier. Lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens. 3-64 chars. Used as filesystem directory name.

description.description: Short description shown in the character library browser.

author.description: Plain string for simple cases; object form when the author wants a URL attached.

version.description: Semantic version. Must be bumped on every redistribution.

license.description: SPDX license identifier. v0.1 accepts a fixed set of common permissive/creative-commons licenses. LICENSE file content must match.

tags.description: Free-form tags for library search and filtering.

renderer.description: Renderer type. 'sprite' = frame sequence, 'live2d' = Cubism 4 model. See ADR-000 for licensing notes.

defaultState.description: State loaded immediately on character activation. Must be a key of states.

states.description: State name → StateDefinition. Must include at least 'idle' and 'happy' (minimum viable character).

emotionOverrides.description: Character-specific text → texture overrides. Keys are literal strings or simple regex fragments the runtime searches for in event.text. Higher priority than default texture patterns.

state.description: Optionally override the dominant state instead of (or in addition to) the texture.

path.description: Relative path inside the package to the directory containing frame_NN.webp|png files. Must remain inside assets/states/.

fps.description: Target playback framerate. The state machine uses this for timing; renderer may decouple actual render FPS.

loop.description: If true, the animation loops while the state is active. If false, the animation plays once and transitions to `then` (or falls back to 'idle' if `then` is omitted).

then.description: State to transition to after a non-looping animation completes. Must match a key of the parent states object, or 'idle'.

durationMs.description: Hint for the state machine about how long the state should last for non-looping animations. The resolver uses this before scaling by severity.

textures.description: Optional texture-variant asset paths. Keys are canonical texture names. Unknown textures at runtime fall back to the base `path` animation.

blendable.description: Whether the renderer may crossfade OUT of this state. Instant-only states (e.g., extreme warning) can set this to false.

examples.name: Linh
examples.description: Your calm, competent secretary. Watches code with quiet attention.
